title: Prabhavatigupta, the Regent Queen
subtitle: A Gupta Princess Who Ruled the Deccan
region: The Vakataka realm of the Deccan (Vidarbha); a daughter of the Gupta house
moral: Power held with wisdom can steady a realm as surely as any sword.

sources.citation: Poona and Riddhapur copper-plate grants issued in Prabhavatigupta's own name as regent
sources.note: Primary proof of a Gupta princess exercising sovereign authority.

sources.citation: Vakataka genealogical records; her marriage to Rudrasena II
Hans Bakker, The Vakatakas (1997); Upinder Singh (2008)
sources.note: Scholarship on her regency and the Gupta-Vakataka link to Ajanta.

panels.shot: majestic low-angle mid-shot framed by Deccan hills and a distant plateau fortress

dialogue.text: Prabhāvatīgupta, daughter of the Guptas and regent of the Vākāṭakas, holds the Deccan steady between north and south.

panels.shot: broad establishing aerial of the subcontinent with subtle emphasis on north and Deccan

dialogue.text: In the fourth century of the Common Era, the Guptas rise in the north, while the Vākāṭakas guard the Deccan heights.

panels.shot: intimate interior mid-shot of a royal girl in study

dialogue.text: Born to Chandragupta II’s house, Prabhāvatīgupta grows amid poets, priests, and policy, learning how empires breathe.

panels.shot: ceremonial wide-shot of a marriage alliance scene

dialogue.text: She is given in marriage to Rudrasena II of the Vākāṭakas, a living bond tying Gupta north to Deccan south.

panels.shot: mid-shot on palace balcony overlooking Deccan landscape

dialogue.text: In Vidarbha’s forts and fields she learns a new land—the accents, the soil, the fears that ride on its winds.

panels.shot: somber interior wide-shot of royal court with empty throne

dialogue.text: Rudrasena II dies young. The throne of the Vākāṭakas stands bare, while his sons are still children.

panels.shot: low-angle close mid-shot from behind the council looking up at her

dialogue.text: In that hollow of power, the widowed queen steps forward as regent, ruling in her sons’ name and in her own.

panels.shot: close-up on her hands holding a copper-plate grant

dialogue.text: Copper plates from Poona and Riddhapur will later tell her story, for they bear her orders, not another’s.

panels.shot: thoughtful profile close-up as she listens to petitioners

dialogue.text: She grants land to Brāhmaṇas and institutions, binding frontier villages into the king’s law with plate and seal.

panels.shot: rear three-quarter shot of her overseeing scribes at a long table

dialogue.text: Write this clearly: these lands are granted under my regency.

panels.shot: medium shot, slightly low angle, as she addresses skeptical nobles

dialogue.text: Some bristle at a woman’s hand upon the realm, yet the copper plates call her mahādevī, born of the Gupta gotra.

panels.shot: interior close-up of an engraved line on a plate, her reflection faint on the metal

dialogue.text: She names herself of the Dhāraṇa Gupta line, letting all know the northern empire stands behind her rule.

panels.shot: wide rural shot of her chariot arriving in a village

dialogue.text: Year after year, she steadies outlying lands, hearing disputes, remitting dues, weighing justice in unfamiliar dialects.

panels.shot: montage wide-shot of artisans and monks near rocky cliffs

dialogue.text: Under the Vākāṭakas, the Deccan’s wealth will one day carve sanctuaries like Ajanta into living rock.

panels.shot: quiet interior mid-shot of her at a shrine

dialogue.text: Her grants favor Śaiva shrines and Brāhmaṇa settlements, weaving devotion, learning, and land into one fabric.

panels.shot: gentle interior wide-shot of the maturing princes with her in the background

dialogue.text: Her sons grow into their inheritance while she keeps the throne warm, never letting the kingdom see a crack.

panels.shot: transition wide-shot of court as she steps back from the central seat

dialogue.text: Around the middle of the fifth century, the regency fades, but the order she set in place endures.

panels.shot: close-up on her aging face looking at a stored copper plate

dialogue.text: Let them remember that a woman’s hand once signed these lands.

panels.shot: timeless wide-shot blending her figure with a backdrop of Ajanta-like caves and a map

dialogue.text: Prabhāvatīgupta’s reign of ink and will binds Gupta and Vākāṭaka, proving that wisdom at the center can hold a realm as firmly as any sword.
